items.title: Air Pollution’s Daily Pulse Over the Northeast
items.tldr: The TEMPO mission helped scientists track morning nitrogen dioxide that contributed to afternoon ozone along the New York–Washington corridor in May 2026.

items.title: How an astrophysicist uses Codex to help simulate black holes
items.tldr: Discover how astrophysicist Chi-kwan Chan uses Codex to build black hole simulations, helping scientists study extreme physics and test Einstein’s theory of general relativity.

items.title: Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem
items.tldr: OpenAI supports the EU Code of Practice on AI content transparency, advancing provenance standards and tools to help people understand AI-generated content.

items.title: Access OpenAI models and Codex through your Oracle cloud commitment
items.tldr: Access OpenAI models and Codex through Oracle Cloud, using existing commitments to build and deploy AI with enterprise security and governance.

items.tldr: NASA has selected multiple small businesses for the Western Regional Multiple Award Construction Contract, which supports a broad range of facility enhancement, modernization, and sustainment work at NASA’s Armstrong…

items.tldr: If you doubted his genius, doubt no more.

items.title: NASA, NOAA to Hold Joint Session at 23rd Symposium on Operational Environmental Satellite Systems
items.tldr: Abstracts are now being accepted for the session, which will take place at the 2027 AMS Annual Meeting.

items.title: Reroute, Don't Remove: Recoverable Visual Token Routing for Vision-Language Models
items.tldr: Vision-language models (VLMs) project images into hundreds to thousands of visual tokens, making decoder inference expensive in both attention computation and KV-cache memory. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Context-Driven Incremental Compression for Multi-Turn Dialogue Generation
items.tldr: Modern conversational agents condition on an ever-growing dialogue history at each turn, incurring redundant attention and encoding costs that grow with conversation length. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: FACTR 2: Learning External Force Sensing for Commodity Robot Arms Improves Policy Learning
items.tldr: Contact-rich manipulation requires force sensitivity, but many robot arms lack dedicated force sensors due to their high cost. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: DIRECT: When and Where Should You Allocate Test-Time Compute in Embodied Planners?
items.tldr: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are increasingly deployed as high-level planners for embodied agents, with an emerging strategy of scaling test-time compute to improve capability. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Redesign Mixture-of-Experts Routers with Manifold Power Iteration
items.tldr: Router is the cornerstone component to the Mixture-of-Experts models. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: System Report for CCL25-Eval Task 5: New Dataset and LoRA-Fine-Tuned Qwen2.5
items.tldr: Recently, large language models (LLMs) have achieved promising progress in the fields of classical Chinese translation and the generation of classical poetry. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Deficient executive control in transformer attention
TAHOE: Text-to-SQL with Automated Hint Optimization from Experience
items.tldr: Large Language Models (LLMs) have democratized database access through Text-to-SQL, but moving from prototypes to production remains difficult. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: ATLAS: Active Theory Learning for Automated Science
items.tldr: Advancing scientific understanding through mechanistic modeling requires posing the right experimental questions to yield maximally informative data. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: APPO: Agentic Procedural Policy Optimization
items.tldr: Recent advances in agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) have substantially improved the multi-turn tool-use capabilities of large language model agents. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: SPEA2$^+$: Improved Density Estimation in SPEA2 with Provable Runtime Guarantees
items.tldr: The Strength Pareto Evolutionary Algorithm 2 (SPEA2) is a popular and prominent evolutionary algorithm for solving multi-objective optimisation problems. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Illumination-Robust Camera-Based Heart-Rate Estimation for Physiological Sensing in Robots
items.tldr: Physiological awareness is important for service, social, and assistive robots that interact with humans in everyday environments. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: UniIntervene: Agentic Intervention for Efficient Real-World Reinforcement Learning
items.tldr: Human-in-the-loop reinforcement learning (HiL-RL) has emerged as an effective paradigm for real-world robotic manipulation, enabling online policy improvement with human guidance. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
items.tldr: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key component in modern large language models, yet the rollout stage remains the key bottleneck in RL training pipelines. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Ambient Diffusion Policy: Imitation Learning from Suboptimal Data in Robotics
items.tldr: We propose Ambient Diffusion Policy, a simple and principled method for imitation learning from suboptimal data in robotics. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: On Subquadratic Architectures: From Applications to Principles
items.tldr: Transformers dominate modern sequence modeling, but their quadratic attention incurs substantial computational cost. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Latent World Recovery for Multimodal Learning with Missing Modalities
items.tldr: We study multimodal learning under missing modalities, with particular motivation from bioscience applications in which heterogeneous modalities are often only partially available when decisions need to be made. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Anatomy of Post-Training: Using Interpretability to Characterize Data and Shape the Learning Signal
items.tldr: Language-model post-training is the main stage at which model behavior is shaped, yet it still largely involves optimization of scalar rewards that summarize diverse desiderata. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: CHORUS: Decentralized Multi-Embodiment Collaboration with One VLA Policy
items.tldr: Multi-robot collaboration allows robots to efficiently take on a wide range of tasks, from moving a couch through a doorway to assembling structures on a construction site. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: PRC-linked influence operations are targeting AI debates in the US
items.tldr: S.  tech debates, data center narratives, tariffs, and false claims about ChatGPT.

items.title: Nonslop: A Gamified Experiment in Human-AI Collaborative Writing
items.tldr: The rapid proliferation of large language models (LLMs) raises critical questions about human creativity and individual expression in an era of AI-assisted creation. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Atlas H&E-TME: Scalable AI-Based Tissue Profiling at Expert Pathologist-Level Accuracy
items.tldr: Hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining is the cornerstone of histopathology, yet scalable, quantitative analysis of H&E whole-slide images (WSIs) remains a central challenge in computational pathology. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Claw-SWE-Bench: A Benchmark for Evaluating OpenClaw-style Agent Harnesses on Coding Tasks
items.tldr: General-purpose agents such as OpenClaw are increasingly used as autonomous tool users, but their coding ability is difficult to measure under SWE-bench: a generic agent does not by itself satisfy the clean Docker… (arXiv preprint)

items.title: ALIGNBEAM : Inference-Time Alignment Transfer via Cross-Vocabulary Logit Mixing
items.tldr: Domain fine-tuning degrades the safety of large language models: fine-tuned specialists readily comply with harmful prompts framed in domain language. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Adjoint Method versus Physics-Informed Neural Networks in PDE-Constrained Inverse Problems
items.tldr: Inverse problems governed by partial differential equations (PDEs) are central to computational mechanics and are commonly solved by adjoint-based optimization, while physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) have… (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Fourier Features Let Agents Learn High Precision Policies with Imitation Learning
items.tldr: High-precision robotic manipulation requires fine-grained spatial reasoning that is often difficult to achieve with RGB-only policies due to depth ambiguity and perspective scale issues. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Measuring Semantic Progress in Multi-turn Dialogue via Information Gain
items.tldr: Evaluating multi-turn dialogue is challenging because quality emerges across turns rather than within individual responses. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: NASA Equips Astronauts, Industry with Robotic Intelligence
items.tldr: As NASA plans long-term missions on the Moon, the agency could use robots to perform routine tasks, allowing crew members to dedicate more time to science and exploration.  However, robotic motion control requires…

items.title: A Pfaffian quantum Hall state of ultracold bosons
items.tldr: Fractional quantum Hall states are a cornerstone of topological physics, hosting fractionally charged quasiparticles with exotic statistics that promise to enable topologically protected quantum information processing. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Collective neutrino oscillations: Many-body non-forward effects and non-classicality
items.tldr: Neutrino evolution in dense astrophysical environments is typically described either within a quantum kinetic framework, which neglects the build-up of multi-body correlations, or through simplified many-body… (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Diabetes org apologizes for ejecting scientists over criticism of Trump
items.tldr: For days after the stunning incident, the ADA had doubled-down on the choice.

items.title: Scaling-optimal purification of noisy qubit unitary channels
items.tldr: We consider the problem of purifying noisy qubit unitary channels. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: The Simplified Stabilizer ZX-Calculus is Minimal
items.tldr: The stabilizer fragment of the ZX calculus is amongst the most important fragments of the theory. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Train Ride to NASA Kennedy for Artemis III Booster Segments
items.tldr: The final booster motor segments for NASA’s SLS (Space Launch System) rocket that will help propel Artemis III astronauts on their journey to space shipped from Northrop Grumman’s Railyard Shipping Facility in Corinne,…

items.title: Fermions are fundamentally more nonlocal than Bosons
items.tldr: Bell's theorem shows that entangled quantum particles can exhibit correlations that classical particles cannot reproduce without an additional nonlocal resource, such as communication. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Gate-tunable spin-valley transport via carrier velocity in monolayer WSe$_2$
items.tldr: We theoretically investigate spin- and valley-resolved quantum transport in monolayer tungsten diselenide (WSe$_2$) described by an effective massive Dirac Hamiltonian. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Entanglement generation between field modes mediated by a fluctuating conducting wall
items.tldr: We consider a movable conducting plate of finite mass, between two fixed ones, whose mechanical degrees of freedom are treated quantum-mechanically and bound to its equilibrium position by a harmonic potential. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Quantum repeater segment with free-space coupled co-trapped ions using telecom photon interference
items.tldr: A quantum repeater segment is a basic building block of a quantum repeater, generating buffered entanglement of quantum memories to connect quantum repeater cells. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Partitioned Iterative Quantum Scheduling of Satellites for Urgent Disaster Response: Case study of Wildfire
items.tldr: The standard in Earth-observation tasks today is having near real-time access to surface images in response to changing conditions. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Super-Link Fragility in Asymmetric W-Class States under Quantum Noise
items.tldr: The asymmetric three-qubit W-class state $|\overline{W_3^L}\rangle$ defines an isosceles entanglement-network geometry, (a) two vertex-base (VB) links form stronger bipartite connections, (b) while the base-base (BB)… (arXiv preprint)

items.title: An iterative Ising decoder for quantum error correction codes
items.tldr: The Ising framework maps the decoding problem in quantum error correction onto ground-state optimization of a classical Hamiltonian, in which $X$-$Z$ error correlations enter as cross terms. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: A post-selected quantum model of cosmic acceleration
items.tldr: The origin of cosmic acceleration remains a central problem in cosmology, commonly attributed to a cosmological constant within the $Λ$CDM model or to dynamical dark energy. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Multipartite reference-frame-independent quantum cryptographic communication
items.tldr: Reference frame mismatch among communication parties introduces errors in quantum cryptographic protocols. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Substrate insulated Josephson junctions for superconducting quantum circuits
items.tldr: We have developed a fabrication technique for Josephson junctions that employs a three-dimensional patterned, low-loss substrate instead of commonly used organic resists. (arXiv preprint)

items.title: Superconductivity in the pressure-amorphized topological insulator CrP$_4$
items.tldr: The interplay among superconductivity, magnetism, and nontrivial band topology represents one of the most compelling frontiers in condensed matter physics. (arXiv preprint)
